verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is canonically cytosolic but achieves documented plasma membrane surface exposure in three defined non-baseline contexts: (1) heat shock stress recovery with lipid-anchored embedding, (2) cancer-cell-restricted surface display linked to aggressive phenotype, and (3) immunogenic cell death. The protein embeds in the PM via interaction with intracellular phosphatidylserine and is the target of cell-surface-directed cancer therapeutics. Surface accessibility is restricted to these stress/oncogenic/death-state contexts, not baseline.
